data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260507T002014-5164f53a/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260507T002014-5164f53a/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260507T002014-5164f53a/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260507T002014-5164f53a/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260507T002014-5164f53a/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-d3aca2b7">
    <title>test: cover task execution cleanup and resource exhaustion end-to-end</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The cleanup/resource contract needs an end-to-end regression harness, not only isolated unit tests. The May 6 failure crossed command, git worktree, tracker claim, resource classification, and operator-output boundaries: a unit-only suite could still miss the exact behavior where ddx work claims the next bead after ENOSPC.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:686-718 owns real git worktree setup/cleanup, while cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:437-530 owns queue drain loop state; existing tests cover many individual branches but not the full resource-exhausted incident path.
- cli/cmd/try.go:306-342 and cli/cmd/work.go:101 route command contexts into workers, but there is no command-level e2e test proving cleanup/resource failures produce correct operator output and tracker state.
- cli/internal/agent/run_state_test.go:95-145 tests orphan recovery shapes, but not full DDx temp root cleanup plus registered worktree pruning plus claim safety.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:449-498 requires cleanup to be inline, loop-level, background, observable, and loop-fatal on resource exhaustion.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add end-to-end tests that use a temp project, temp DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR, real git worktree commands where feasible, and injectable resource-check failures where filling a filesystem would be unsafe.
- Verify tracker state after failure: no extra bead claimed, failed bead open/re-claimable, no retry-after cooldown for host resource exhaustion.
- Verify cleanup state after failure: stale unregistered dirs and stale registered worktrees are removed; active/preserved evidence survives.
- Verify operator output and loop events contain cleanup summary and resource_exhausted stop reason.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not create a real ENOSPC by filling /tmp or exhausting inodes in CI.
- Do not require live harness/model/provider calls.
- Do not test unrelated production-reachability bead content.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestWorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_StopsBeforeNextClaim runs ddx work against two ready beads with injected resource_exhausted setup failure and verifies only the first bead is touched.
2. TestWorkCleanupEndToEnd_PrunesStaleRegisteredAndUnregisteredWorktrees creates real stale DDx worktrees/dirs under a temp DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR and verifies ddx work or ddx cleanup prunes them safely.
3. TestTryResourceExhaustionEndToEnd_Reclaimable verifies ddx try on a target bead fails with resource_exhausted, exits non-zero, and leaves the bead open/unclaimed without execute-loop-retry-after.
4. TestCleanupEndToEnd_PreservesPublishedEvidence verifies complete .ddx/executions and .ddx/runs bundles plus refs/ddx/iterations/* are not removed.
5. TestBackgroundCleanupEndToEnd_UsesLockAndJitter verifies periodic cleanup can be driven deterministically with fake time and does not run concurrent destructive passes.
6. The e2e tests use temp directories and injectable fakes rather than consuming real disk/inodes.
7. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestWorkResourceExhaustionEndToEnd|TestWorkCleanupEndToEnd|TestTryResourceExhaustionEndToEnd|TestCleanupEndToEnd|TestBackgroundCleanupEndToEnd" -count=1 passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <notes>
decomposed into ddx-07f2709c, ddx-5ed78486, ddx-83f1f0af, ddx-4fcd9427, ddx-b2047bfb
    </notes>
    <labels>phase:2, area:test, area:agent, area:cli, kind:test, reliability, spec:FEAT-010, spec:SD-025, cleanup, resource-exhaustion, e2e</labels>

  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>

</execute-bead>
